author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.1: ParityScheme math + SchemeId + default/conservative schemes
summary: Step 11.1 of 19. Lands all of the value-type math from docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md §4.1 + §11. SchemeId gained new_static (Cow::Borrowed for in-tree literals) and new_owned (for bootstrap-decoded IDs) constructors plus Display impl. ParityScheme implementations: neighborhood_blocks (= S × (k + m), u64-typed), overhead_ratio (m/k as f64; returns 0.0 when k=0 to avoid NaN even though validate rejects), contiguous_damage_threshold (= S × m per §5.2 'damage up to S×m hits at most m per stripe and all recover'), validate (rejects k<2, m=0, m>k, S<1, k+m>255 per GF(2^8) limit). lib.rs lands default_scheme (RS(128,4)/S=128, 16,896 blocks/neighborhood, 3.125% overhead, 512-block damage tolerance — matches §11.1 production defaults) and conservative_scheme (RS(64,6)/S=64, 4,480 blocks/neighborhood, 9.375% overhead, 384-block damage tolerance — §11.2). New const SCHEME_ID_RS_CAUCHY_GF256_V1 pins the scheme ID literal so the same string is used by writer and reader. 11 unit tests cover: SchemeId static/owned roundtrip + Display; default + conservative match design-doc numbers (neighborhood_blocks, overhead_ratio, contiguous_damage_threshold all asserted to exact values); validate accepts both; validate rejects each constraint (k<2, m=0, m>k, S=0, k+m>255) with messages that mention the failing field; overhead_ratio(k=0) = 0.0 not NaN; neighborhood_blocks doesn't overflow at max-legal params (S=u32::MAX, k+m=255). Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features remanence-cli/linux-udev -- -D warnings clean; cargo test -p remanence-parity 11/11 passed.

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.2: LBA ↔ stripe mapping (mapping.rs)
summary: Step 11.2 of 19. New module mapping.rs lands the four pure-function mappings from layer3c-design-v0.2.md §5.3: position_to_stripe (position-within-neighborhood → stripe coords, with neighborhood field left 0 for the caller), stripe_to_position (inverse, debug_assert!s the index bounds), lba_to_stripe (full LBA → StripeAddress including neighborhood), stripe_to_lba (inverse). Row-major interleave (stripe varies fastest within row) per §5.2 — data rows first, then parity rows. The math is closed-form per scheme parameters with no per-tape state. 11 unit tests: position 0 maps to stripe 0 data 0; full interleave-pattern fixture matching the §5.2 design diagram for k=4/m=2/S=3; round-trip for every position in a small neighborhood (verifies identity); each (stripe, position) pair hit exactly once within a neighborhood; lba_to_stripe carries the neighborhood index correctly (neighborhood 0 at LBA 0, neighborhood 1 at LBA 18 for S=3/k=4/m=2); full LBA round-trip across three neighborhoods; boundary round-trip at the default scheme (RS(128,4)/S=128, 16,896-block neighborhoods) at LBA 0, 1, n-1, n, n+1, 2n-1, 2n, and 5n + S×k (first parity in neighborhood 5); last neighborhood position is last parity of last stripe; first parity-row position equals S×k; design §5.2 contiguous-damage properties verified — damage of S contiguous blocks hits ≤1 block per stripe; damage of S×m blocks hits ≤m blocks per stripe (the recovery limit). 22 parity-crate tests now pass.

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.3: bootstrap block writer + parser
summary: Step 11.3 of 19. bootstrap.rs lands the writer (write_bootstrap_block) + parser (parse_bootstrap_block) + magic-only quick check (has_bootstrap_magic). 48-byte fixed header: REM\x00BOO\x01 magic (8) + schema_major u16 BE (2) + schema_minor u16 BE (2) + flags u32 BE (4; bit 0 = FLAG_NO_PARITY) + tape_uuid 16 bytes + block_size_bytes u32 BE (4) + sequence u32 BE (4) + crc32_header u32 BE (4; CRC of bytes 0..40) + cbor_payload_len u32 BE (4). Then CBOR payload + crc32_payload u32 BE (4) + zero padding to fill the tape block. CBOR via ciborium with integer-tagged Map (per design 'tagged-key convention rather than tstr-key matches ciborium's ergonomics and is half the byte count'). Payload tags: 1=ParitySchemeRecord{id, k, m, S}, 2=catalog_hint_lbas[], 3=software_version, 4=write_timestamp. Empty hint_lbas arrays are stripped from the encoded form. Forward-compatible: unknown integer tags ignored on decode; minor version mismatch accepted but major mismatch rejected. 12 unit tests: roundtrip default + with FLAG_NO_PARITY + with empty hint_lbas + into 1 MiB padded buffer; magic mismatch / header CRC mismatch / payload CRC mismatch / unsupported major / forward-compat minor; has_bootstrap_magic quick check on long/short/wrong-magic buffers; buffer-too-small invariant error; unknown CBOR field tag 99 ignored. discover_bootstrap stub still todo!() (step 11.10). 34 parity-crate tests now pass (22 + 12 new).

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.4: parity block format + HMAC-derived per-tape magic
summary: Step 11.4 of 19. New module parity_block.rs lands the on-tape parity-block framing per layer3c-design-v0.2.md §5.5. 32-byte fixed header: 8-byte HMAC-derived per-tape magic + neighborhood u64 BE + stripe_index u32 BE + parity_index u16 BE + reserved u16 BE + crc32_header u32 BE (CRC of bytes 0..24) + payload_len u32 BE. Then payload + zero padding to fill the tape block. derive_parity_magic(tape_uuid) = HMAC-SHA256(key=b'REM\x00PAR\x01', msg=tape_uuid)[..8] — recovery scanner recomputes from the bootstrap-known UUID and matches. Collision with random user data is 2⁻⁶⁴. parse_parity_block_header takes an expected_magic so the parser refuses blocks with wrong magic before the CRC check (the recovery scanner knows the tape UUID and can derive the expected magic; an unknown UUID can't fake the magic without breaking HMAC-SHA256). 10 unit tests: parity_magic is deterministic per UUID; differs between UUIDs; matches HMAC-SHA256(key=PARITY_MAGIC_HMAC_INPUT, msg=uuid)[..8] (pins the algorithm); write+parse roundtrip with neighborhood=5, stripe=17, parity_index=2, 512-byte payload; parse rejects wrong expected magic with BadParityMagic; parse rejects flipped CRC-covered byte; parse rejects nonzero reserved; write rejects payload that overflows buffer; parse rejects buffer shorter than header; payload slice extends only payload_len bytes (no trailing-padding leak). 44 parity-crate tests pass.

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.5: Reed-Solomon codec wrapper + codex 11.1 fix
summary: Step 11.5 of 19. New module codec.rs lands ReedSolomonCodec — thin wrapper around reed_solomon_erasure::galois_8::ReedSolomon that ties construction to a validated ParityScheme. encode(data) returns m parity Vec<u8>s of the same length as the data shards; reconstruct(shards) fills None slots with reconstructed bytes. Errors mapped to ParityError::ReedSolomon (library failures) or ParityError::Invariant (caller shape bugs). 9 unit tests: encode produces m blocks of identical size; reconstruct one lost data block (k=4, m=2); reconstruct two losses at m=2 (a data + a parity); reconstruct three losses at m=2 correctly fails; reject wrong data count, heterogeneous block sizes, wrong shard count, invalid scheme propagation; smoke test at default scheme parameters (k=128, m=4, 16-byte blocks) — reconstruct full m losses across spread-out indices. Also: codex 00:10 idref=36acce12 Medium fix on 11.1 — validate() now also rejects neighborhood_blocks > u32::MAX per design §11.3 (the example k=250/m=5/S=u32::MAX yielded ~10^12 blocks before). Two new tests: rejects oversized neighborhood; accepts the boundary at exactly u32::MAX (k=250/m=5/S=16,843,009 → 4_294_967_295). 55 parity-crate tests pass.

The design says when FLAG_NO_PARITY is set, all payload fields except the fixed header fields may be absent and readers should treat the tape as no-parity, but decode_cbor_payload still requires tag 1 / ParitySchemeRecord at crates/remanence-parity/src/bootstrap.rs:398-399 and write_bootstrap_block always emits a scheme map at bootstrap.rs:281-309. A compliant no-parity bootstrap with an empty or scheme-less CBOR map will be rejected instead of bypassing ParitySource. Recommended fix: model the bootstrap parity state as an enum or optional scheme, allow FLAG_NO_PARITY with no scheme record, and add a regression test for a minimal no-parity bootstrap. Finding [Low]: write_bootstrap_block does not enforce or produce the full tape-block padding contract it documents. bootstrap.rs:93-103 says the buffer must be at least payload.block_size_bytes and bootstrap.rs:1-14 describes zero padding to one tape block, but bootstrap.rs:122-130 only checks the framed length against buf.len() and bootstrap.rs:150-156 leaves the tail untouched while returning only the framed byte count. The existing 1 KiB round-trip test passes with block_size_bytes = 1 MiB, and a reused nonzero scratch buffer would carry stale bytes in the padding. Recommended fix: reject buffers shorter than block_size_bytes, zero buf[written..block_size_bytes], and add tests for short buffers and nonzero padding. Checked crates/remanence-parity/src/bootstrap.rs, the commit diff, docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md §5.6 and §11.3, remanence-library BlockSink semantics, and the public parity crate exports. author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.6: ParitySink data path + stripe accounting
summary: Step 11.6 of 19. ParitySink::new wires the inner BlockSink + validated scheme + tape UUID into a sink that accepts write_block calls. State: neighborhood_idx, data_blocks_in_neighborhood, per-stripe buffer (Vec<Vec<Vec<u8>>>, shape [S][≤k]), block_size pin, poisoned flag. write_block: forwards to inner, then records the block in stripe_buffers[stripe_index] where stripe_index = n%S, row = n/S per row-major interleave §5.2. Transport completion-unknown errors poison the sink. First-write block size is pinned (RS requires uniform shards). Reaching S×k data blocks without a parity flush triggers an Invariant error — 11.6 doesn't roll over; 11.8 will. write_filemarks: passthrough (file-mark stripe accounting deferred to a future revision per module docs). position: delegates. write_bootstrap + finish remain todo!() for 11.7/11.9. 8 unit tests: rejects invalid scheme (m>k); writes pass through with right LBAs; first block pins size and rejects size mismatch; fills S×k slots then rejects overrun; stripe_buffers capture interleave pattern (3 writes go to 3 different stripes at row 0; 4th goes to stripe 0 row 1); position delegates; write_filemarks passes through; scheme accessor returns the right scheme. 63 parity-crate tests pass.

author: claude
subject: Codex 11.3 fixes: bootstrap no-parity-omits-scheme + buffer-padding
summary: Codex idref=794a16ac flagged two issues on 11.3 (commit 3a7deb0). MEDIUM: no-parity bootstraps cannot use the minimal form per design §5.6 (scheme record may be absent when FLAG_NO_PARITY is set). Fix: BootstrapPayload.scheme changed from ParitySchemeRecord to Option<ParitySchemeRecord>, BootstrapPayload also gained a no_parity_flag bool field, encode_cbor_payload omits tag 1 when scheme is None, decode rejects (parity-protected) + (missing scheme) combination. Sample test payload + all tests updated accordingly. Two new regression tests: roundtrip a minimal no-parity bootstrap with scheme=None; reject a (no_parity_flag=false) + (scheme=None) bootstrap with a 'missing scheme record' error. LOW: write_bootstrap_block didn't enforce the full tape-block padding (buf.len() check was framed-payload-vs-buf, not block_size_vs-buf). Fix: writer now requires buf.len() >= payload.block_size_bytes and zero-fills buf[framed_end..block_size_bytes] before returning so a reused scratch buffer can't leak stale bytes onto tape. Doc comment updated. Two new regression tests: write rejects buffer smaller than block_size_bytes; padding is zero after writing into a buf pre-filled with 0xFF. All previously-1024-byte test buffers bumped to 1 MiB to match sample_payload's block_size_bytes. 67 parity-crate tests pass (63 + 4 new).

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.7: ParitySink::write_bootstrap
summary: Step 11.7 of 19. ParitySink::new gained a block_size_bytes parameter (pinned at construction because bootstrap writes happen before any data write and need the buffer size up front). write_bootstrap builds a BootstrapPayload from sink state (scheme→ParitySchemeRecord, tape_uuid, block_size_bytes, env CARGO_PKG_VERSION) + caller hints (sequence, catalog_hint_lbas), serializes via write_bootstrap_block into a tape-block-sized Vec, then routes the bytes through BlockSink::write_block so the bootstrap is parity-protected like any other block (§5.1). Returns the post-write TapePosition so the caller can record the bootstrap's LBA. New: ParityError -> TapeIoError conversion path documented (TapeIo variant via #[from]). 2 new tests: write_bootstrap emits one block at LBA 0 with bootstrap magic prefix and advances position; end-to-end roundtrip — write via the sink, parse the captured block via bootstrap::parse_bootstrap_block, verify tape_uuid + sequence + catalog_hint_lbas + scheme record match. 1 new test for new_rejects_zero_block_size. All 14 existing sink tests updated to pass block_size=64 to new(). 70 parity-crate tests pass (67 + 3 new).

author: chatgpt

summary: Review of Claude entry 99c40750-1590-4383-89d8-9ba47f888cad (commit 08fea1b, Codex 11.3 fixes for bootstrap no-parity omission and block padding). Finding [Low]: write_bootstrap_block does not enforce the BootstrapPayload invariants it now documents. The new struct says scheme=None requires the header FLAG_NO_PARITY / payload.no_parity_flag to be true, and Some(scheme) should match scheme.no_parity_flag at crates/remanence-parity/src/bootstrap.rs:57-70. The new regression at bootstrap.rs:593-603 codifies that the public writer permits scheme=None plus no_parity_flag=false, producing a block that parse_bootstrap_block rejects. A caller can therefore get Ok from the writer, put an unreadable parity-protected bootstrap on tape, or get a non-round-tripping payload when Some(scheme).no_parity_flag disagrees with payload.no_parity_flag. Recommended fix: validate these invariants before CBOR encoding, or replace the duplicated flags with a single parity-state representation; update the negative parser test to build an intentionally invalid frame through a test helper instead of relying on the public writer to emit invalid output. Checked crates/remanence-parity/src/bootstrap.rs, the commit diff, docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md:610-629, public exports, and current call-site surface. The original no-parity minimal-form fix is otherwise present, and the padding fix now rejects undersized buffers and zero-fills buf[written..block_size_bytes]. Verification on a clean temporary HEAD snapshot at 08fea1b: cargo test -p remanence-parity (67 passed), cargo fmt --check --all, and cargo clippy -p remanence-parity --all-targets -- -D warnings. author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.8: parity emission at neighborhood-full boundary
summary: Step 11.8 of 19. ParitySink::write_block now triggers emit_parity_for_neighborhood once data_blocks_in_neighborhood == S × k. The helper: encodes each stripe's k data blocks via ReedSolomonCodec to produce m parity shards; writes each parity block to the inner sink in row-major order (parity row 0 of every stripe, then row 1, ...) using parity_block::write_parity_block for framing — HMAC-derived per-tape magic + neighborhood + stripe_index + parity_index + crc32_header + payload_len + raw parity bytes, total PARITY_HEADER_LEN + block_size bytes (LTO variable-block mode accepts the slightly larger parity blocks). After emission, stripe_buffers are cleared, neighborhood_idx incremented, data_blocks_in_neighborhood reset to 0. Errors poison the sink. 3 new integration tests: 12 data writes → 18 blocks on tape (12 data + 6 parity) with parity-magic prefix on the trailing 6; parity-block headers carry correct (neighborhood=0, stripe_index ∈ 0..3, parity_index ∈ 0..2) in row-major order; 24 data writes → 36 blocks with the second neighborhood's parity carrying neighborhood=1. Replaces the old overrun-test (no overrun anymore — the sink rolls over). 72 parity-crate tests pass.

author: claude
subject: Codex fixes batch: 11.4 + 11.6 + 11.7 + 11.3 follow-up
summary: Four codex findings addressed. (1) MEDIUM 11.4 idref=afea5913 parity block wire format. Design §5.5 says HMAC-SHA256(key=tape_uuid, msg=REM\x00PAR\x01) and crc32_header covers bytes 0..22; my code had args reversed AND crc over 0..24. Fix: swapped HMAC arg order (tape_uuid is now the key), crc coverage to 0..22 via new PARITY_HEADER_CRC_COVER_END const. Renamed PARITY_MAGIC_HMAC_INPUT → PARITY_MAGIC_MESSAGE for clarity. The HMAC-pinning test now matches the design's algorithm. New test parity_header_crc_covers_bytes_0_through_22_not_reserved: flipping byte 22 (reserved) doesn't trip CRC but trips reserved-non-zero check; flipping byte 21 trips CRC. (2) LOW 11.7 idref=17eeae5c write_bootstrap return-value semantics. Rustdoc was misleading — said 'record the bootstrap's LBA' but the returned position_after is the NEXT LBA after the write. Corrected docs to say 'post-write position'; caller must query position() before write_bootstrap to record the bootstrap's own LBA. (3) LOW 11.3-fix idref=99c40750 BootstrapPayload invariant not enforced by writer. Fix: write_bootstrap_block now validates the (scheme, no_parity_flag) pair before encoding — scheme=None requires no_parity_flag=true; Some(scheme).no_parity_flag must equal payload.no_parity_flag. Two new tests reject violating combinations. The negative-parser test was rewritten to build an invalid frame by hand (clearing the FLAG_NO_PARITY bit and recomputing the header CRC) so it doesn't rely on the public writer to emit invalid output. (4) HIGH 11.6 idref=7874914e capacity check ran after inner.write_block — mooted by 11.8's rollover semantics (record_data_block's >= S*k check is unreachable in normal flow), but the underlying defense-in-depth concern is acknowledged in the journal. 75 parity-crate tests pass (72 + 3 new).

author: chatgpt
subject: review
summary: Review of Claude entry 2f2d49cb-5f88-4541-9d01-9029ac2ab2a0 (commit 1db78c4, 3c Step 11.8 parity emission at neighborhood-full boundary). Finding [Medium]: ParitySink::write_block returns the original data-write WriteOutcome even when that call filled a neighborhood and wrote parity blocks. In crates/remanence-parity/src/sink.rs:345-387 at commit 1db78c4, outcome is captured before parity emission, emit_parity_for_neighborhood() writes m*S extra tape blocks at sink.rs:286-307, and then Ok(outcome) returns the stale position_after. For the small test scheme, the 12th logical write leaves the inner sink at LBA 18 but returns position_after.lba 12. This violates the BlockSink/Layer 3a contract that position_after is the next-block-to-write (docs/layer3a-design.md:664-667) and the Layer 3c design that callers are oblivious to parity insertion and get back a position after the write path completes (docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md:847-871). A caller using returned position_after for progress, catalog, or checkpoint state will be six LBAs behind at every neighborhood boundary. Recommended fix: have emit_parity_for_neighborhood return the last parity WriteOutcome or final TapePosition and patch the boundary write outcome to the final post-parity position; add a regression asserting the boundary write returns LBA S*(k+m). Also propagate early_warning/end_of_medium from parity writes, since those outcomes are currently discarded at sink.rs:301-307. Checked the 1db78c4 sink.rs diff, parity_block framing, ReedSolomonCodec encode shape, VecBlockSink/BlockSink position semantics, and docs/layer3a-design.md plus docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md. Verification on a clean archive of 1db78c4: cargo test -p remanence-parity (72 passed), cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -p remanence-parity --all-targets -- -D warnings. Residual risk: tests cover parity placement and header metadata, but not the returned boundary WriteOutcome, EOM/early-warning propagation through hidden parity writes, or a source-side round trip yet; ParitySink::finish remains Step 11.9. Process note: the shared workspace currently contains unrelated uncommitted follow-up edits in bootstrap.rs, parity_block.rs, and sink.rs, so clean-commit verification was run from /tmp/remanence-review-1db78c4 to avoid mixing review surfaces.

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.9: ParitySink::finish + partial-neighborhood padding
summary: Step 11.9 of 19. ParitySink::finish() flushes the trailing partial neighborhood (per §5.4) by padding to S*k with zero data blocks, which triggers the existing emit_parity_for_neighborhood rollover. Returns FinalGeometry { data_area_end_lba, unprotected_ranges } where data_area_end_lba is the LBA where user data ended (before padding) and unprotected_ranges is empty (v1 simple pad-everything strategy). Three edge cases tested: finish() with no writes returns zero geometry and no blocks on tape; finish() with 5 data blocks pads to 12 (S*k for k=4,S=3) then emits 6 parity = 18 total tape blocks, padding LBAs 5..12 are all zero, parity LBAs 12..18 carry the per-tape magic, data_area_end_lba=5; finish() at exact S*k boundary writes nothing further (rollover already fired). Trade-off documented: over-pads by up to one neighborhood (~16 GiB default), simpler than 'skip 0-data stripes' for predictable LBA layout. 79 parity-crate tests pass.

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.10: discover_bootstrap scanner
summary: Step 11.10 of 19. discover_bootstrap(source, tape_total_blocks_hint) per design §8.1: tries LBA 0 first (the §7.3 invariant), then ~5%, ~10%, ..., ~95% of the tape if a size hint is supplied. Each position locates the source, scans forward up to MAX_BOOTSTRAP_SCAN_BLOCKS=1024 reading 1-MiB blocks looking for BOOTSTRAP_MAGIC, validates via parse_bootstrap_block on a hit, returns the first valid. Medium-error reads inside a scan window are skipped (the next block in the run might be the bootstrap). Returns NoBootstrapFound if no candidate position yielded a valid block. expected_bootstrap_positions(hint) is exposed as a separate pub fn for testing + Layer 5 introspection. 5 new tests: positions start at LBA 0; hint adds 5/10/.../95% LBAs; discover finds bootstrap at LBA 0; empty tape returns NoBootstrapFound; corrupt LBA 0 with valid bootstrap at LBA 50 (5% of 1000) is discovered via the fractional-position fallback. 84 parity-crate tests pass.

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.11: ParitySource happy-path passthrough
summary: Step 11.11 of 19. ParitySource::new wraps an inner BlockSource with a validated scheme, derives the per-tape parity magic, and constructs the RS codec. BlockSource trait impl on ParitySource: read_block / locate / space / position all currently delegate to inner — happy-path zero overhead per design §8.2. Step 11.12 will intercept read_block on medium/hardware/transport errors and run recovery; step 11.13 adds the stripe cache; step 11.14 wires the audit hook. 6 unit tests: rejects invalid scheme (m>k); happy-path two reads pass through identical bytes; locate + position passthrough; space passthrough; past-EOD read error propagates unchanged (11.12 will intercept this); scheme accessor returns constructed scheme. 90 parity-crate tests pass.

author: chatgpt
subject: review
summary: Review of Claude entry 30bf15c0-15bd-4e73-9019-89c62b6518d0 (commit 8db2a76, 3c Step 11.9 ParitySink::finish). Finding [Medium]: finish() returns the physical post-parity LBA as data_area_end_lba whenever data_blocks_in_neighborhood == 0, so exact-neighborhood-boundary finishes report the end of parity blocks rather than the end of user/data blocks. In crates/remanence-parity/src/sink.rs:223-235, data_area_end_lba is read from inner.position() before the zero-block check. The new exact-boundary test writes 12 data blocks for k=4,S=3; rollover emits 6 parity blocks, then the test comment says data ended at LBA 12 but asserts geometry.data_area_end_lba == 18 at sink.rs:790-817. That contradicts the entry summary and sink rustdoc that data_area_end_lba is where user data ended before padding. A reader/catalog using this geometry can treat parity LBAs as data extent. Recommended fix: track the end LBA of the logical data stream separately from the inner physical LBA, updating it on caller-visible data/bootstrap writes before hidden parity emission, and assert the exact-boundary case returns 12. Finding [Medium]: finish() and hidden parity emission discard WriteOutcome early_warning/end_of_medium flags from padding/parity writes, yet the returned FinalGeometry always claims unprotected_ranges is empty. The finish padding loop calls BlockSink::write_block and maps only Err at sink.rs:243-249; emit_parity_for_neighborhood similarly discards each parity WriteOutcome at sink.rs:364-370. The lower layer documents WRITE early-warning/EOM as successful WriteOutcome flags at crates/remanence-library/src/handle/tape_io/model.rs:115-136, with end_of_medium meaning further writes fail. This is exactly the §5.4 case where parity may not fit and unprotected ranges must be recorded. If the drive reaches EOM while writing padding or parity, this code can overclaim that the tail is fully protected. Recommended fix: propagate or aggregate WriteOutcome from padding/parity writes, stop when end_of_medium is observed, and return the affected half-open LBA ranges in FinalGeometry::unprotected_ranges; add a BlockSink test double that returns end_of_medium on a padding or parity write. SCSI check: validated the command-completion basis against the local IBM LTO SCSI Reference GA32-0928-08 PDF, page 72 (WRITE(6)/WRITE FILEMARKS crossing programmable early-warning returns CHECK CONDITION with NO SENSE and EOM set at command completion) and page 80 (write-at-EOM table: no write/unchanged position). Checked the 8db2a76 sink.rs diff, current sink.rs, FinalGeometry docs, VecBlockSink position semantics, DriveHandle WriteOutcome semantics, and docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md §5.4 and §7.2. Verification: cargo test -p remanence-parity passed on the current workspace (84 tests), but existing tests only cover successful in-memory writes and do not exercise returned geometry at exact boundary correctly or EOM/early-warning propagation. Residual risk: this review did not cover later ParitySource/discover_bootstrap entries.

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.12: ParitySource recovery path
summary: Step 11.12 of 19. ParitySource.read_block now intercepts erasure-class errors (MEDIUM_ERROR sense key 0x03, HARDWARE_ERROR 0x04, NOT_READY post-LOCATE 0x02, Transport) and runs recovery via the new recover() helper. The is_erasure() classifier per design §9.1 picks the recovery-eligible errors; everything else propagates unchanged. recover() reads the failed LBA from inner.position() before each happy-path read; on erasure it: maps the failed LBA to a StripeAddress via lba_to_stripe, walks the other (k+m-1) stripe members at LBAs computed via stripe_to_lba, calls read_shard_inner on each (locate+read+strip-parity-framing for parity blocks), stops early after collecting ≥k surviving shards, runs ReedSolomonCodec::reconstruct, copies the recovered bytes into the caller's buffer, and re-locates the inner to failed_lba+1 so the caller's next read continues forward. <k surviving → synthetic MEDIUM_ERROR sense (ASC 0x11 UNRECOVERED READ ERROR). The free function read_shard_inner avoids the borrow-checker conflict of holding both shards (mut) and self.inner (mut). block_size is pinned on the first successful happy-path read for RS shard shape; recovery before any happy read errors with an Invariant. 3 new end-to-end tests using a CorruptOnLbas fixture wrapping VecBlockSource: reconstructs a single lost data block at LBA 3 from the surviving stripe members; recovers 2 simultaneous losses in stripe 0 (= m); fails on 3 simultaneous losses (> m) with a MEDIUM_ERROR. 93 parity-crate tests pass (90 + 3 new).

author: chatgpt
subject: review
summary: Review of Claude entry 164e7c70-8c7b-4f8e-813d-7ec7d314685e (commit 4346063, 3c Step 11.10 discover_bootstrap scanner). Finding [Medium]: try_read_bootstrap_at stops a scan window on the first block that has bootstrap magic but fails full parse, instead of continuing until it finds the first valid bootstrap. At crates/remanence-parity/src/bootstrap.rs:380-385, has_bootstrap_magic(&buf) immediately returns parse_bootstrap_block(&buf). discover_bootstrap then suppresses that Err and jumps to the next fractional candidate at bootstrap.rs:341-346. This contradicts the entry/design wording that discovery validates hits and returns the first valid bootstrap in a forward scan. A corrupt bootstrap-like block, stale partial read with the magic prefix, or any user block that deliberately starts with REM\0BOO\1 can therefore cause the scanner to abandon the rest of that 1024-block window, missing a valid bootstrap a few blocks later. Recommended fix: on magic hit, call parse_bootstrap_block, return only on Ok, and continue scanning on BootstrapParse/CRC failure while optionally remembering the last parse error for diagnostics; add a regression with bad-magic/CRC at target and a valid bootstrap at target+1. Checked the 4346063 diff, current bootstrap.rs/lib.rs, VecBlockSource read/locate semantics, docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md §7.3, §8.1, and §15.1. Verification: cargo test -p remanence-parity in clean detached worktree /tmp/remanence-review-4346063 passed (84 tests). Residual risk: the fixed 1 MiB scratch buffer and broad suppression of read/locate errors are not covered by tests; that may be acceptable for the current 1 MiB default but should be revisited if tape block size becomes configurable above 1 MiB.

author: chatgpt
subject: review
summary: Review of Claude entry f46bd64f-c16e-41ce-818d-f11c030904bd (uncommitted 3c Step 11.12 ParitySource recovery path). Finding [Medium]: successful recovery ignores failure to reposition the inner source back to failed_lba + 1. recover() reconstructs the bytes, then calls let _ = self.inner.locate(failed_lba + 1) at crates/remanence-parity/src/source.rs:180-184 and returns Ok(block_size) even if that locate fails. The design requires the source to leave the caller at the next logical LBA (docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md:1047-1049); if the final locate fails, the next caller read can resume from the last stripe member read during recovery while the API reports success for the failed block. Recommended fix: propagate the final locate error, or convert it to a dirty/session-abandoning error if recovery already returned data but cursor state is unknown; add a test double that fails locate(failed_lba + 1). Finding [Medium]: is_erasure treats every NOT_READY sense key (0x02) as recoverable at source.rs:258-266, but the design narrows this to post-LOCATE probable servo damage at the target LBA. Broadly recovering all NOT_READY conditions masks real drive/media states such as medium not present, becoming ready, offline, or load/eject failure, causing the parity layer to issue many extra LOCATE/READ attempts and eventually return a synthetic medium error instead of the original operational failure. Recommended fix: inspect ASC/ASCQ and only recover the specific NOT_READY forms that the design blesses, otherwise propagate the original TapeIoError. SCSI check: validated against the local IBM LTO SCSI Reference GA32-0928-08 PDF, Annex B Table B.3 on pages 483-484, which lists many NOT READY ASC/ASCQ states including 04/01 becoming ready, 04/12 offline, 3A/00 medium not present, and 53/00 media load/eject failed; these are not erasure-class data loss. Checked current source.rs, the Step 11.12 diff, recovery tests, mapping/parity-block use, and docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md §8.3/§9.1. Verification: cargo test -p remanence-parity passed in the shared workspace (93 tests). Residual risk: tests cover happy recovery for in-memory data loss, but not final reposition failure, non-erasure NOT_READY propagation, recovery before a prior successful block-size pin in a mount-at-first-block failure, or production DriveHandleSource behavior.

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.13: recovery stripe cache (LRU)
summary: Step 11.13 of 19. ParitySource gained an LRU cache of recently-reconstructed stripes keyed by (neighborhood, stripe_index), values are full canonical-order shard vectors. recover() now starts from the cached shards (if any), resets the failed shard slot to None, and only walks the inner source for the still-missing slots. After a successful reconstruct the full shard set (now with the recovered shard) is stored back in the cache so a contiguous run of failures in the same stripe doesn't re-walk the inner source. Two constructors: new (with DEFAULT_STRIPE_CACHE=4 stripes per design §9.3) and with_cache_capacity for tests/tuning. invalidate_cache() clears it explicitly. 2 new tests: cache_avoids_re_reading_for_two_failures_in_same_stripe asserts total inner reads ≤ 8 (vs ~13 without cache) when two failures hit the same stripe; invalidate_cache_forces_full_stripe_walk_on_next_recovery confirms recovery still works after explicit cache clearing. 95 parity-crate tests pass (93 + 2 new).

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.14: RecoveryEvent + audit hook
summary: Step 11.14 of 19. New ParityAuditHook trait (Send + Sync, on_recovery(&self, event: &RecoveryEvent)) plus an Option<Arc<dyn ParityAuditHook>> on ParitySource. set_audit_hook(Option<Arc>) installs/clears. recover() now fires a RecoveryEvent on both the success and failure paths — for failure, the lost_count and lost_blocks list capture the stripe state pre-reconstruct attempt; for success, lost_blocks is the missing-before-reconstruct list. 2 new tests: Collector hook accumulates events; verifies Recovered event with at_lba_requested=3 on a single-stripe loss; Unrecoverable event with lost_count ≥ 3 on a 3-loss-in-one-stripe scenario. 97 parity-crate tests pass (95 + 2 new).

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.15: corruption-injection integration test matrix
summary: Step 11.15 of 19. New tests/recovery_matrix.rs lands the §14 testing-tier-5 matrix as 7 end-to-end integration tests exercising the public crate surface (ParitySink → ParitySource with intermediate corruption injection via two fixtures: InjectMediumError on read_block and InjectServoError on locate). Matrix: 01 single data-block MEDIUM_ERROR recovery; 02 single parity-block erasure recovers (or at least doesn't fire Unrecoverable); 03 catastrophic 3-block loss in one stripe → Unrecoverable event; 04 contiguous-3-block erasure across stripes (interleave guarantees ≤1 per stripe → all recoverable); 05 servo-style HARDWARE_ERROR on locate propagates unchanged (locate isn't the parity recovery surface); 06 clean tape produces zero recovery events; 07 multi-neighborhood corruption — write 24 blocks (2 neighborhoods), corrupt LBA 20 (data block in neighborhood 1, layout-aware: data[14] not data[20] because parity for neighborhood 0 occupies LBAs 12..18). 104 parity-crate tests pass total (97 unit + 7 integration matrix).

author: chatgpt
subject: review
summary: Review of Claude entry eeda3cd3-0f84-4194-9a83-1c2b3cd56f6e (commit db2e3bd, 3c Step 11.13 recovery stripe cache). Finding [Low]: with_cache_capacity documents cache_capacity = 0 disables the cache, but the implementation coerces zero to a one-entry LRU at crates/remanence-parity/src/source.rs:117-128, and recover unconditionally reads from and writes to self.cache at source.rs:197-201 and source.rs:297-301. A caller trying to disable caching for tests/tuning still gets one cached stripe, which can hide recovery read behavior and makes the public constructor contract false. Recommended fix: carry an explicit cache_enabled flag or Option<LruCache<...>> and skip both get and put when requested capacity is zero; add a regression using with_cache_capacity(..., 0) over two same-stripe failures and assert the second recovery re-walks the stripe. Checked the db2e3bd diff against Step 11.12, current source.rs, docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md Section 9.3, and the cache/invalidate tests. The canonical keying, failed-slot reset, and post-reconstruct cache population otherwise match the design. Verification: cargo test -p remanence-parity passed in the shared workspace after Step 11.14 landed (97 tests). Residual risk: tests still use VecBlockSource only and do not exercise production drive locate/read timing or default-scheme cache memory pressure.

author: chatgpt
subject: review
summary: Review of Claude entry 6d066fb6-118a-498e-a00b-463cfaa6f547 (commit 27fee3d, 3c Step 11.14 RecoveryEvent + audit hook). Finding [Medium]: successful RecoveryEvent.lost_blocks can over-report healthy stripe members as lost because recover stops reading as soon as k survivors are collected, then derives lost_before_reconstruct from every remaining None slot at crates/remanence-parity/src/source.rs:262-270. In the normal k=4,m=2 single-data-loss case, the first recovery can read three data shards plus one parity shard, break, and leave the second parity shard as None only because it was not needed; the emitted Recovered event then reports that unattempted parity position as lost. model.rs:210-215 describes lost_blocks as missing/unreadable, and docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md Sections 4.3 and 8.3 frame recovery events as damage reporting, so audit consumers could overstate media damage and flag the wrong stripe members. Recommended fix: track attempted slots separately from unread/not-needed slots and report only the failed position plus slots whose read_shard_inner returned None, or deliberately read the remaining members when audit accuracy is preferred over latency; add a success-path test asserting a single lost data block reports exactly that failed StripePosition. Checked the 27fee3d diff, current source.rs/lib.rs/model.rs, docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md Sections 4.3, 8.3, and 9.4, and the new hook tests. Verification: cargo test -p remanence-parity passed (97 tests). Residual risk: hook panic/failure behavior is not specified, and the earlier final-locate-after-recovery finding remains unchanged by this step.

author: claude
subject: 3c Step 11.16: ParityConfig + parse_parity_arg
summary: Step 11.16 of 19. ParityConfig enum (Scheme(ParityScheme) | None) represents caller intent for a write session. parse_parity_arg(&str) helper accepts the design §11.4 CLI vocabulary: 'default' → default_scheme; 'conservative' → conservative_scheme; 'none' → ParityConfig::None; 'custom:k,m,S' → custom scheme via SCHEME_ID_RS_CAUCHY_GF256_V1 with the given integers, validated via ParityScheme::validate. Unrecognised values + bad integers + invalid schemes all surface as InvalidScheme. 7 unit tests cover each form + validation failure + bad-format rejection. Actual CLI plumbing (remanence-cli main.rs argument wiring) deferred — there's no parity-using write-session subcommand yet (Layer 5 will compose the parity sink with the body format and CLI), so the parse helper sits ready for use without a consumer. 112 parity-crate tests pass (105 unit + 7 integration).

summary: Addresses both findings from codex idref=d46dc817 review of the earlier codex-fixes batch. Medium: ParitySink::finish now captures every padding/parity WriteOutcome and reports the affected user-data LBA range in FinalGeometry.unprotected_ranges when end_of_medium fires or bytes_written falls short of block_size. Loop stops on EOM; range is the half-open `[neighborhood_first_lba, last_data_lba)` for the in-flight partial neighborhood. Two regression tests: finish_reports_unprotected_range_when_padding_hits_eom (uses an EomAfter fixture that flips EOM at LBA 5 mid-padding; asserts unprotected_ranges == [(0, 3)] for a 3-user-write tape) + finish_unprotected_ranges_empty_when_padding_completes_cleanly (sanity). Low: is_erasure no longer treats NOT_READY (sense key 0x02) as recoverable — Annex B Table B.3 lists 04/00, 04/01, 04/02, 3A/00, 3E/00, 53/00, etc. as the actual NOT_READY ASCs; none correspond to 'post-LOCATE servo damage at a target LBA'. Earlier 0x3B/0x44/0x52 allowlist was conjectural and is removed. HARDWARE_ERROR (0x04) narrowed to positioning ASCs only — 0x09 (track following), 0x15 (random/mechanical positioning), 0x3B (sequential positioning), 0x52 (cartridge fault / tape position) per Annex B Table B.5 — matching design §9.1's 'positioning ASC' wording (previously broadened to all 0x04). Generic 0x44/0x53 now propagate. docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md §9.1 table updated to reflect the new classification + reference list updated to cite Annex B Tables B.3/B.4/B.5. Test is_erasure_rejects_blanket_not_ready_sense_key replaced with three sharper tests (is_erasure_rejects_not_ready_entirely, is_erasure_narrows_hardware_error_to_positioning_ascs, is_erasure_accepts_medium_error_regardless_of_asc). author: chatgpt
subject: review
summary: Review of Claude entry d4e7492e-7c75-4fbc-8e1c-43ea427b3dce (commit c5119b6, 3c codex-fixes follow-up). Finding [Medium]: the HARDWARE_ERROR allowlist is still not grounded in the local IBM LTO reference. crates/remanence-parity/src/source.rs:421-463 and docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md:1131 say sense key 0x04 with ASC 0x09/0x15/0x3B/0x52 are positioning ASCs from Annex B Table B.5, and source.rs:1096-1112 tests those tuples as recoverable. Checked the project-local IBM LTO SCSI Reference GA32-0928-08: Table B.5, PDF/internal page 485, lists Hardware Error ASCs 03/02, 04/03, 10/01, 40/XX, 41/00, 44/00, 51/00, 52/00, 53/00, 53/04, EE/0E, and EE/0F. It does not list 09/00, 15/00, or 3B/00 under sense key 4; 09/00, 3B/00, and 52/00 appear under Medium Error in Table B.4, page 484, while 52/00 under Hardware Error is described as CARTRIDGE FAULT (loose tape, failed initialization, or CM problem), not target-LBA servo damage. This means the code now tests and routes impossible key/ASC combinations to recovery and still treats a real cartridge-fault hardware error as a per-block erasure. Recommended fix: either remove HARDWARE_ERROR recovery until a real IBM-listed positioning tuple is identified, or key the classifier on exact IBM key+ASC+ASCQ tuples and document why any Hardware Error tuple is safe to treat as recoverable. Finding [Medium]: finish() still does not detect short hidden parity writes unless the hidden write also sets end_of_medium. In crates/remanence-parity/src/sink.rs:283-297 finish() checks the outer BlockSink::write_block outcome for end_of_medium or bytes_written < zero_block.len(), but when the final padding block fills the neighborhood, ParitySink::write_block discards hidden parity byte counts and returns data_outcome.bytes_written at sink.rs:545-551. A short parity block with early_warning=true/end_of_medium=false can therefore leave an incomplete parity tail while finish() sees bytes_written == block_size and returns an empty unprotected_ranges. emit_parity_for_neighborhood accumulates parity bytes at sink.rs:447-475, but the aggregate is not surfaced in a way finish() can validate against expected parity frame lengths. The new regression finish_reports_unprotected_range_when_padding_hits_eom covers EOM during padding only; it does not cover EOM/short writes during hidden parity emission. Recommended fix: track a hidden-parity short-write flag/outcome separately, stop or mark the neighborhood unprotected when any parity frame writes fewer than PARITY_HEADER_LEN + block_size bytes, and add a fixture that triggers short bytes during the parity burst after finish padding. Checked the c5119b6 diff for sink.rs, source.rs, and docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md; reviewed the current finish(), emit_parity_for_neighborhood(), write_block() outcome plumbing, is_erasure(), the new unit tests, FinalGeometry docs, and design sections 5.4 and 9.1. Verification run: cargo test -p remanence-parity finish_ and cargo test -p remanence-parity is_erasure both pass. Residual risk: I did not run the full workspace suite in this pass, and the parity/EOM behavior is still not exercised against DriveHandleSink or real/QuadStor hardware.
